About

Stephen Morgan MP

Stephen Morgan is the first ever Labour Member of Parliament (MP) for Portsmouth South, a seat he took with a 21.5% swing in the 2017 General Election.

He was returned again in the General Election in 2019 more than trebling the majority and increasing the Labour share of the vote in the constituency by the largest in the country at 7.6%.

In 2024, Stephen was again re-elected as the MP for Portsmouth South, after more than doubling his majority to 13,155.

Stephen, who was born and bred in the city he serves, studied at local schools and was the first in his family to go to university, graduating from the University of Bristol with an BSc (Hons) in 2002 and a postgraduate Masters of the Arts (MA) from London in 2003.

Stephen Morgan MP

Stephen Morgan is the first ever Labour Member of Parliament (MP) for Portsmouth South, a seat he took with a 21.5% swing in the 2017 General Election.

He was returned again in the General Election in 2019 more than trebling the majority and increasing the Labour share of the vote in the constituency by the largest in the country at 7.6%.

In 2024, Stephen was again re-elected as the MP for Portsmouth South, after more than doubling his majority to 13,155.

Stephen, who was born and bred in the city he serves, studied at local schools and was the first in his family to go to university, graduating from the University of Bristol with an BSc (Hons) in 2002 and a postgraduate Masters of the Arts (MA) from London in 2003.

Active local campaigner

He started his working life in local government working across the Chief Executive’s Office, strategy, democratic, and community involvement sections at Portsmouth City Council and from 2008-2015 was Head of Community Engagement at the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea where he oversaw a £3m investment programme in communities.

He has been Chief Executive of a medium-sized charity delivering infrastructure and volunteering support for the not-for-profit sector and delivering services including a centre for rough sleepers and targeted support for carers and young people.

In civic life he has been the chair of governors at Arundel Court Junior School, a trustee of Age UK Portsmouth and vice-chair of governors at Priory School. He is a longstanding community activist with a wide range of knowledge and understanding of local issues and needs. His interest in culture and regeneration led him to be the chair of the Portsmouth Cultural Consortium for many years.

In 2016 he was elected onto Portsmouth City Council representing Charles Dickens ward and within five months elected the Labour Group Leader.

Active local campaigner

He started his working life in local government working across the Chief Executive’s Office, strategy, democratic, and community involvement sections at Portsmouth City Council and from 2008-2015 was Head of Community Engagement at the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea where he oversaw a £3m investment programme in communities.

He has been Chief Executive of a medium-sized charity delivering infrastructure and volunteering support for the not-for-profit sector and delivering services including a centre for rough sleepers and targeted support for carers and young people.

In civic life he has been the chair of governors at Arundel Court Junior School, a trustee of Age UK Portsmouth and vice-chair of governors at Priory School. He is a longstanding community activist with a wide range of knowledge and understanding of local issues and needs. His interest in culture and regeneration led him to be the chair of the Portsmouth Cultural Consortium for many years.

In 2016 he was elected onto Portsmouth City Council representing Charles Dickens ward and within five months elected the Labour Group Leader.

Strong national voice

In September 2025, Stephen was appointed as Lord Commissioner of the Treasury in the Government Whips’ Office. In the role, Stephen will be working across Government, to renew Britain and deliver the change people voted for.

The Portsmouth MP previously served as the Minister for Early Education following Labour’s general election victory. In the role, Stephen was responsible for delivering better childcare and early education to give every child in the country the best start in life.

In opposition, Stephen served on Labour’s frontbench as Shadow Minister for Rail (September 2023 – July 2024), helping form the party’s plans to fix Britain’s railways and bring them into public ownership.

Stephen was the Shadow Minister for Schools (September 2021-September 2023), working to ensure children are given the equal opportunity to thrive.

He also served as Shadow Armed Forces Minister (April 2020-December 2021), standing up for Armed Forces Communities across the country, including those in the heart and home of the Royal Navy, Portsmouth.

Prior to this, he was Shadow Minister for Local Government (Communities) from July 2019 to April 2020, with a policy brief to speak at the despatch box on services from adult social care, children’s services, faith and community cohesion, welfare reform and debt services to community pubs.

He also held the position of Private Parliamentary Secretary (PPS) for the Shadow Secretary of State for Communities and Local Government from July 2017-September 2019, and sat on the Public Accounts Committee, the oldest select committee in Parliament, focusing on defence, from November 2017-July 2019.

He has a keen interest in improving public services, encouraging educational opportunities for all and tackling crime.

Strong national voice

In July 2024, Stephen was appointed the Minister for Early Education following Labour’s general election victory. In the role, Stephen is responsible for delivering better childcare and early education to give every child in the country the best start in life.

The Portsmouth MP previously served on Labour’s frontbench as Shadow Minister for Rail (September 2023 – July 2024), helping form the party’s plans to fix Britain’s railways and bring them into public ownership.

Stephen was the Shadow Minister for Schools (September 2021-September 2023), working to ensure children are given the equal opportunity to thrive.

He also served as Shadow Armed Forces Minister (April 2020-December 2021), standing up for Armed Forces Communities across the country, including those in the heart and home of the Royal Navy, Portsmouth.

Prior to this, he was Shadow Minister for Local Government (Communities) from July 2019 to April 2020, with a policy brief to speak at the despatch box on services from adult social care, children’s services, faith and community cohesion, welfare reform and debt services to community pubs.

He also held the position of Private Parliamentary Secretary (PPS) for the Shadow Secretary of State for Communities and Local Government from July 2017-September 2019, and sat on the Public Accounts Committee, the oldest select committee in Parliament, focusing on defence, from November 2017-July 2019.

He has a keen interest in improving public services, encouraging educational opportunities for all and tackling crime.

Here to help

Stephen is bringing politics back to the high street with his constituency office. Any constituent can pop by to his office and see him or his team with their ideas, issues or concerns. The office is open for enquiries Monday to Thursday 10am to 11.30am and 2pm to 3.45pm. A regular advice centre, appointments and home visits are also available.

Get in touch

E-mail: stephen.morgan.mp@parliament.uk
Telephone: 023 9229 1088
Facebook: facebook.com/StephenMorganMP
